seoTitle: Freeburgh vs French Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Freeburgh and French Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Freeburgh vs French Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Freeburgh and French Island.
heading: Freeburgh vs French Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Freeburgh, Victoria and French Island, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to French Island (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Freeburgh or French Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Freeburgh has a flood risk score of 23/100, while French Island scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Freeburgh scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. French Island scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Freeburgh or French Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
